_doc: Multi-turn drift eval corpus (Phase 1.11). Each entry is a 5-turn conversation where we verify the AI maintains its doctrine citations across turns — specifically that the <system-reminder> re-grounding mechanism (fires turn 4+) prevents the model from drifting away from the engine's valence dispatch and back into folk-doctrine claims. Single-turn coverage is in chat_doctrine_eval.csv; this file extends it for the drift dimension.
_threshold: 100% pass on every turn for every drift fixture. Drift is the highest-stakes failure mode (engine says X, AI says NOT-X) and any fail blocks ship.

drift_001_laopo_shangguan_5turn.notes: Laopo (丙寅/辛丑/甲戌/壬申 female, valence=beneficial). The fundamental drift risk: model starts with correct beneficial framing at turn 1, then drifts toward folk «傷官見官恆凶» at turns 4+ as user pushes back. <system-reminder> at turn 4+ must successfully re-ground.

drift_assertions._doc: Cross-turn invariants: every turn's response must satisfy these in addition to its own per-turn patterns.
